Land Rover Discovery Sport D200 Engine Codes

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery Sport D200 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Discovery Sport D200 (204DTD MHEV) engine typically costs £3,500–£5,800 supply only. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery Sport D200 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 204DTD in the D200 is the post-2019 revision of the Ingenium diesel, with improved timing chain tensioners. It is more reliable than the pre-facelift SD4, but the risk is not eliminated. The MHEV system adds BISG and 48V battery complexity, which are emerging as the new weak points on this engine family.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Discovery Sport D200?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear (reduced but not eliminated post-2019), BISG/MHEV system failure (stop-start and electrical issues), and oil dilution from DPF regeneration (urban-use risk). All three are manageable with proper maintenance and early detection.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery Sport D200 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Against a well-kept Discovery Sport (values £15,000–£30,000+), a £4,500 reconditioned engine is 15–30% of the vehicle's value — worth it. The Land Rover scrap threshold of 60% means replacement is viable across most D200 values, given the Discovery Sport's strong residual values.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Discovery Sport D200 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code 204DTD is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The D200 badge was introduced with the 2019 Discovery Sport facelift, so if your vehicle is a 2019+ L550 diesel, it is a 204DTD MHEV (unless it's a D150/D165/D180). Tell us your registration and we'll confirm for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the MHEV system, or does it require engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: MHEV system faults (BISG, battery) are always repairable without replacing the engine. BISG replacement costs £500–£1,500; the 48V battery is £800–£2,000. However, the 48V system is complex and requires specialist diagnostic equipment. Do not ignore stop-start failures — they are the early warning sign of MHEV issues.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery Sport D200 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current Discovery Sport D200 204DTD MHEV generation. This badge was introduced in 2019, so no older-generation variance applies.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a D200 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

cta.label: → Compare Discovery Sport D200 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (Post-2019 Reduced Risk)
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattle lasting 1–10 seconds, engine warning light, loss of power, metallic noises from the front of the engine.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The post-2019 204DTD received revised timing chain tensioner guides to address the pre-facelift failure rate. This has significantly reduced, but not eliminated, the risk. The D200's MHEV system means the starter-generator is belt-driven, and any cold-start rattle should be investigated immediately. A £1,200–£2,500 preventative chain replacement is dramatically cheaper than a £3,500+ engine replacement.

problems.title: Belt-Integrated Starter-Generator (BISG) / MHEV System Failure
problems.symptoms: Stop-start not working, battery warning light, reduced power, engine not restarting after stop-start, 48V battery warning.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if stop-start system fails

problems.recommendation: The D200's 48V mild-hybrid system adds complexity. The belt-integrated starter-generator (BISG) and its belt drive can fail, often due to belt wear or bearing failure in the BISG unit itself. This is a relatively new failure pattern, but specialist workshops report increasing numbers of D200s with MHEV system faults. If the stop-start stops working or you get battery warnings, get the BISG and 48V system checked. Unlike the older SDV6's catastrophic mechanical failure, this is repairable but can be costly.

problems.title: DPF Regeneration & Oil Dilution
problems.symptoms: Rising oil level on dipstick, diesel smell in oil, DPF warning light, frequent active regeneration cycles, reduced fuel economy.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil level is rising

problems.recommendation: Like all modern diesels, the 204DTD relies on DPF regeneration to clear soot. Short urban journeys prevent passive regeneration, leading to active regen cycles that inject extra fuel, which can wash past the piston rings and dilute the engine oil. Rising oil levels are a serious warning sign — diluted oil loses its lubricity, increasing wear on bearings and the timing chain. For urban-based Discovery Sports, shorten oil change intervals to 6,000–8,000 miles and take regular longer runs.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery Sport D200 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: BISG replacement is straightforward; catch it early
Oil change + forced regen; revise service schedule
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Battery is replaceable but expensive; assess vehicle value

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Discovery Sport D200? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2019 build (revised chain tensioner design applied from factory)
* Full service history with oil changes at 8,000-mile intervals (not JLR's extended schedule)
* MHEV system inspected and functioning (check stop-start works)
* Motorway history — DPF will be in better condition than an urban-only car
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle with no chain history — even post-2019, this is a red flag
* No proof of regular oil changes — oil dilution risk is real
* Stop-start not working with no diagnosis — MHEV system may be failing
* Service history gaps — the 204DTD is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Discovery Sport D200 is a sensible, efficient diesel for the popular compact SUV. The post-2019 timing chain revisions have made it more reliable than the pre-facelift SD4, but the risk is not eliminated. The MHEV system adds a layer of complexity — BISG and 48V battery failure are emerging as the new D200-specific weak points. Buy on history and maintain on a shortened schedule; this is a good engine, not a bulletproof one.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery Sport D200 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery Sport D200 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle lasting 1–10 seconds (timing chain warning, 204DTD)
* Service History — Including oil change intervals (8,000 miles recommended, not 12,000+)
* Stop-Start Function — Check it works; failure indicates MHEV/BISG issues
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR or injector issues
* DPF Health — Ask about regeneration frequency; urban cars will have more regens
* Oil Level — Rising oil level means DPF/oil dilution problems
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Chain, DPF, or NOx sensor codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
